title: Primer design using NIH's Primer-BLAST tool.
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4rlsq","text":"This protocol demonstrates the basic usage of the Primer-BLAST tool in order to design PCR primers from a given FASTA sequence.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":50,"length":12}],"data":{}},{"key":"5e0rl","text":"The results should show the sequence of at least one pair of primers (forward and reverse) within the chosen parameters.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"7AB0317FF88711EE99240A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/tools/primer-blast/index.cgi"}}}}
steps: Open your internet browser (Google Chrome recommended).
Screenshot of the PCR Template section with the corresponding text box. Copy and paste your FASTA sequence in the text box or upload a FASTA file using the ¨Choose File¨ button.

Screenshot of the Primer Parameters and Exon/intron selection sections. Enter the optimal parameters for your primers. Depending on your application, the PCR product size and the primer Melting temperatures are extremely important values!
You can select a custom range (i.e. the positional value of the first and last nucleotide of your ROI) for the forward and reverse primers.
Screenshot of the Get Primers button. Click on the ¨Get Primers¨ button.
creator: Francisco Franco García Calderon
